operations.notes:
Updates the Internet referral configuration for the device.
If there was no previous configuration, it will be created.

responseMessages.message: Internet referral configuration was updated
Device with given ID does not exist
Internet referral object can only be configured for StoneSoft (except master engine) or Check Point SMC/CMA devices
Internet referral configuration was malformed
Access is denied.
Internet referral configuration is returned
Device with given ID does not exist
Internet referral object can only be configured for StoneSoft (except master engine) or Check Point SMC/CMA devices
Internet referral configuration was not found for device ID
Access is denied.
Internet referral configuration was deleted
Device with given ID does not exist
Internet referral object can only be configured for StoneSoft (except master engine) or Check Point SMC/CMA devices
Internet referral configuration for device ID does not exist
Access is denied.

operations.summary: Create new Internet representation for a device
operations.notes:
The internet referral lets you represent the Internet as an object on a device.
See REST API: /internet_referral/{deviceId:[0-9]+}/object for a description of how internet referral is resolved.

responseMessages.message: Internet referral configuration was created
Device with given ID does not exist
Internet referral object can only be configured for StoneSoft (except master engine) or Check Point SMC/CMA devices
Internet referral configuration was malformed
Internet referral configuration for given device ID already exists
Access is denied.

operations.summary: Get resolved Internet representation for device
operations.notes:
The order of precedence used to resolve the Internet object is:
Step 1: Identify the Internet referral:
Search for a defined Internet referral on the specified device.
If it is not found on the device, search the parent and grandparents (up the device tree) for a defined Internet referral.
If a defined Internet referral is found, then match the name in the Internet referral to a network object, as defined in Step 2.
If an Internet referral is not found, it resolves to "Any".
Step 2: Resolve the object by name:
Search the specified device for a defined network object that matches the name returned in Step 1.
If a matching network object is not found, an error will be returned.

responseMessages.message: Resolved Internet referral object for device is returned
Device with given ID does not exist
Internet referral object can only be configured for StoneSoft (except master engine) or Check Point SMC/CMA devices
Configured object was not found on the device
Access is denied.

timeout.description: The timeout value for the service in seconds

minSource.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the minimum source port number, ICMP Service: the ICMP type number.

maxSource.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the maximum source port number.
